retry_reason: Failure occurs when no matching orig_ifinfo exists or the router's refcount is zero (object dying); in both cases retrying will not succeed without external state changes, and such changes are not transient in normal operation.
contract_summary: This get-wrapper returns a reference-counted pointer to the batadv_neigh_node (router) associated with a specific outgoing interface. The lookup is performed under RCU protection; the caller receives a reference that must be released via batadv_neigh_node_put when no longer needed. If the function returns NULL, no reference was acquired and the caller must not use the returned pointer. The wrapper safely handles concurrent destruction by using kref_get_unless_zero, ensuring no reference is taken on a dying object.

check.reason: kref_get_unless_zero is called on router->refcount, which is a kref. The path router.refcount.refcount.refs and type chain batadv_neigh_node--kref--refcount_t--atomic_t are consistent with kernel struct definitions and callee info. router is the return value, so location is return index 0.
